title: feuilleea setifera
intro: Feuilleea setifera, also known as the prickly ash or toothache tree, is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly in treating toothache, gum inflammation, and other oral health issues. The medicinal properties of Feuilleea setifera include its ability to reduce pain and swelling, making it a valuable remedy for oral health and potentially other health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. Feuilleea setifera is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and can also be used topically as a poultice or oil to alleviate symptoms.
